data.body:
G3 child-coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 2359 "Surface outbound sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary"), 4-bucket per owned parent criterion, re-cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57). VERDICT: clean pairing — no G3 finding attributable to this child, and (correction below) NO uncovered parent criterion remains across the roster.

OWNED CRITERIA — FULLY covered by this child:
- Parent AC "Outbound sub-op telemetry (links_applied/comments_applied/labels_applied) is surfaced in the structured batch outcome" → 2359 AC1 (surfaces all three in the structured outbound batch outcome + RECON: line, parity with inbound applier). FULLY.
- Parent AC "A `computed > 0 && applied == 0` sub-op condition fails loudly, after the warn-first rollout in Rollout & rollback" → 2359 AC2 (test asserts computed>0 && applied==0 fails, reproducing the link drop) + 2359 AC3 (fail-loud invariant rolls out warn-first behind a flag; promotion to hard-fail and reversion to warn = flag flip, no other code change — exact match to parent Rollout & rollback). FULLY.
- Parent SC "A silent sub-op no-op (e.g. links computed but never applied) fails a test/canary instead of passing green" → 2359 AC2. FULLY.
- Contributes to parent SC "New tests assert behavior/contract … zero change-detector tests added" via 2359 AC5 (canary asserts the count invariant, not specific log strings), and to parent AC "all six child stories closed; full reconciler suite green" via 2359 AC4 (sync semantics unchanged, suite green).

DELEGATION — parent criteria this child does not own are correctly assigned to named siblings; this child's declared OUT items (probe wiring → 822a; applier consolidation → 33d0) are consistent with the roster: AC SSOT-schema/fetcher-validation/differ-validation + SC "producer/consumers cannot drift" → 8937 (+ fe57 fixtures/fake, 59ef verified-fake honesty); AC "collapse to single production path" + AC "retired path deleted" + SC "exercised through production path" → 33d0; AC "bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip asserted hermetically" + AC "every expected-bug probe guard de-encoded" + SC "bidirectional round-trips asserted; no probe encodes a bug as expected" → 822a; AC "one round-trip contract test through the production path using real-Jira fixtures" → 8937 (+ fe57).

CORRECTION TO PRIOR AUDITS ON THIS TICKET — the recurring "Parent AC6 (documented anti-change-detector guardrail) only PARTIALLY covered" finding is a FALSE POSITIVE and is WITHDRAWN. The actual parent acceptance criterion reads: "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B)." It (a) explicitly names its owner — story B = sibling 8937 — and (b) is FULLY covered there: 8937's description states "Owns the epic's guardrail artifact (resolves the roster-level gap): commit a documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests — a short docs/ note (ADR-style) … New tests reference it," and 8937 AC5 = "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail (ADR-style note recording the testing-strategy decision + the schema location + the rules) is committed and referenced by the new tests." Under the G3 ANTI-FP rule (a criterion covered-by-definition by a named consumer counts), this criterion is FULLY covered, not partial. The prior "AC6 PARTIALLY" finding was raised against a paraphrased "governs new tests" wording that does not match the plan text and predates/ignored 8937's explicit ownership; it is not re-filed.

4-bucket summary for this pairing: FULLY — parent "sub-op telemetry surfaced", parent "computed>0 && applied==0 fails loud (warn-first)", parent SC "silent no-op fails canary" (all owned by this child); CONTRIBUTED — parent SC "zero change-detector tests" + parent AC "suite green"; COVERED-BY-NAMED-SIBLING — all schema/contract/applier-collapse/round-trip/probe criteria (8937/fe57/59ef/33d0/822a); STRUCTURAL/AGGREGATE — parent AC "all six closed; suite green". UNCOVERED — none. No G3 coverage finding for this pairing.
